title: Tables and Table widget - ability to fully customise views e.g. suppress or hide group headers
excerpt: It would be very useful if the Table view was fully customisable. For example, it makes sense and is easier to a user to include groups to define related buckets of work. However, as someone who has oversight of all the …

posts.author: BronwynWest (Bronwyn West)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be very useful if the Table view was fully customisable. For example, it makes sense and is easier to a user to include groups to define related buckets of work. However, as someone who has oversight of all the groups of work I need to be able to sort each item by Due date, or priority, or … If I do this now, then I can only see the items sorted by Due date within each group. This means the only way I can get this simple view (outside of a gannt or calendar view which is impossible to use with 200+ items) is to EXPORT the data to excel, delete rows, realign the subitem item (because they export in a different format to the parent item), and do my work there. As a paid product I don’t think you want to be encouraging / needing users to use a secondary software

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @BronwynWest ,
Have you tried to save the filtered views? I’m showing how to do this in his moment in the video 0:40

posts.author: JoshWyatt (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree! I’m looking for the function in the Dashboard view using the Table Widget for the ability to hide boards that return no result from the filter. Currently my Table Widget shows all the boards, regardless if it has a result or not from the filter.

For sake of decluttering, it would be nice to hide these automatically! It would simplify the widget and allow to digest the information faster.

Hide Boards2618×754 87.7 KB

Just me?

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree with both @BronwynWest and @JoshWyatt . I’d like to be able to do both! We use the Table Widget a lot, and it would be very helpful to have settings choices for:

  • Choose order of columns
  • Hide empty tables
  • “All at once” for selecting all columns at the same time across multiple boards displaying in the Table Widget on a Dashboard
